name: Rainbow flag (LGBTQ+ pride)
description: Six equal horizontal stripes — from top to bottom: red, orange, yellow, green, blue and purple — together forming a rainbow.
history: Designed by artist Gilbert Baker, who unveiled the original eight-stripe version at the San Francisco Gay Freedom Day Parade on 25 June 1978. Hot pink and turquoise were dropped over the following year due to fabric availability and to enable an even split when flown on either side of a parade route; the resulting six-stripe version has been in worldwide use as the symbol of LGBTQ+ pride since 1979.

features.symbolism: a rainbow representing the diversity of the LGBTQ+ community
